white.name: White Hat
white.description: Neutral, objective facts, data, and information. No opinions, just what is known and what is missing.
white.example: Our attendance data shows a 12% drop in on-time check-ins this month.

red.name: Red Hat
red.description: Emotions, gut feelings, and hunches, expressed without needing to justify them.
red.example: Honestly, this new policy just feels rushed and it's making people anxious.

black.name: Black Hat
black.description: Critical judgement, risks, weaknesses, and reasons something might fail.
black.example: If we roll this out company-wide without testing, payroll errors could triple.

yellow.name: Yellow Hat
yellow.description: Positive thinking, benefits, value, and reasons an idea could work.
yellow.example: This could actually cut onboarding time in half if we commit to it.

green.name: Green Hat
green.description: New ideas, alternatives, and out-of-the-box possibilities.
green.example: What if new hires got a scavenger hunt instead of a slideshow orientation?

blue.name: Blue Hat
blue.description: Managing the thinking process itself: summarizing, deciding next steps, keeping things on track.
blue.example: Let's park the debate on tools and agree on the timeline first.

easy.sentence: The office coffee machine has been out of order for three days straight.
I have a bad feeling about launching the new client portal on a Friday.
If we skip testing the timesheet export, payroll could send wrong salaries.
This new candidate tracking sheet could save recruiters two hours a day.
What if job interviews started with a two-minute icebreaker game instead of small talk?
Let's agree on today's meeting agenda before anyone brings up side topics.
Forty employees clocked in late this week, according to the attendance sheet.
I'm genuinely excited about the new team-building game, it feels fun.
Rolling this game out without a tutorial will confuse half the new hires.
A gamified onboarding could make first-week retention noticeably higher.

medium.sentence: Client satisfaction scores dropped from 8.2 to 7.1 over the last quarter.
Something about this contract renewal makes me uneasy, even though the numbers look fine.
Automating the vacation-balance calculation might break edge cases for part-time staff.
Automating fuzzy name-matching across the three attendance files could remove hours of manual cleanup weekly.
What if the company ran a mini hackathon where interns pitch automation ideas?
Before we debate features further, let's confirm the deadline and who owns each part.
Recruitment received 340 applications this month, up from 210 last month.
I feel like the team is stretched too thin to take on another client right now.
Signing a client without a clear SLA could lead to scope creep within weeks.
A referral bonus program could meaningfully boost quality applicants next quarter.

hard.sentence: Three departments reported conflicting headcount numbers in the same reporting period.
There's a quiet unease across the tech stream about the new reporting structure, even though no one has said it outright.
Merging the three HR subdomains into one workflow without a transition plan risks losing institutional knowledge.
A unified employee-relations and recruitment dashboard could cut cross-department reporting time by half.
What if job descriptions were co-written live with hiring managers using a shared AI draft instead of a template?
Before restructuring further, let's summarize what each subdomain actually needs and revisit in two weeks.
Exit interview data shows 60% of departures cite unclear growth paths.
It just feels demoralizing to see good employees leave over something we could have fixed.
Promising fast-tracked promotions to retain staff without budget approval could backfire badly.
A transparent internal mobility board could turn retention into a company-wide strength.

easy.title: The Coffee Machine Crisis
easy.scenario_text: The office coffee machine broke down again, and this time IT says it's not worth fixing. Some employees are joking about a 'productivity collapse.' Leadership asked the team to decide what to do about office coffee going forward.

hat_prompts.white: What do we actually know about coffee usage and cost so far?
hat_prompts.red: How do people really feel about losing the coffee machine?
hat_prompts.black: What could go wrong with whatever we decide?
hat_prompts.yellow: What's the upside of any solution we pick?
hat_prompts.green: What's a creative alternative to a standard coffee machine?
hat_prompts.blue: How should the team organize this decision and who decides?

easy.title: The Late Check-In Streak
easy.scenario_text: Attendance data shows a noticeable rise in late check-ins over the past two weeks, right after the new hybrid schedule started. HR wants a team response before it becomes a policy issue.

hat_prompts.white: What does the attendance data actually show?
hat_prompts.red: How might employees feel about being flagged for lateness?
hat_prompts.black: What risks come with tightening attendance rules too fast?
hat_prompts.yellow: What's the benefit of addressing this early?
hat_prompts.green: What unusual approach could fix late check-ins without punishment?
hat_prompts.blue: What's the plan for rolling out any change calmly?

easy.title: The Team-Building Game Pitch
easy.scenario_text: You've pitched a Six Hats mobile game to leadership for team-building, but one manager worries employees will see it as 'just another mandatory app.' The team must respond to this concern together.

hat_prompts.white: What do we know about how similar tools were received before?
hat_prompts.red: What's the gut reaction to being told this is 'mandatory'?
hat_prompts.black: What could make employees resist this game?
hat_prompts.yellow: What's the real benefit for teams who actually engage with it?
hat_prompts.green: What could make the game feel optional and fun instead of forced?
hat_prompts.blue: How do we structure the rollout message to leadership?

easy.title: The Office Snack Budget Cut
easy.scenario_text: Finance just cut the monthly snack budget by 40% to save costs. Some employees are already complaining in the group chat. The team needs to decide how to handle it.

hat_prompts.white: What's the actual size of the budget cut and its cause?
hat_prompts.red: How upset are people really, versus just venting?
hat_prompts.black: What happens if we ignore the complaints?
hat_prompts.yellow: Could a smaller snack budget lead to something better?
hat_prompts.green: What's a fun alternative to replace the old snack routine?
hat_prompts.blue: Who should communicate this decision and how?

easy.title: The Onboarding Slideshow Complaint
easy.scenario_text: New hires keep mentioning in exit surveys that the onboarding slideshow is 'long and boring.' Recruitment wants a fresh take before the next hiring wave arrives.

hat_prompts.white: What exactly do new hires say is wrong with onboarding?
hat_prompts.red: How does a new hire likely feel sitting through it?
hat_prompts.black: What could go wrong if we change onboarding too fast?
hat_prompts.yellow: What would a great first day actually achieve for the company?
hat_prompts.green: What's a completely different way to onboard someone?
hat_prompts.blue: What's the plan to test a new onboarding approach safely?

medium.title: The Fuzzy Name-Matching Bug
medium.scenario_text: The new attendance automation tool mismatched 15 employees last week because their names were entered differently across the three source files (e.g., nicknames, typos). Payroll is asking if it's safe to trust the tool going forward.

hat_prompts.white: What exactly caused the mismatches, and how many were affected?
hat_prompts.red: How does the payroll team feel about trusting an automated tool now?
hat_prompts.black: What's the worst-case risk if this isn't fixed before next payroll run?
hat_prompts.yellow: What's the long-term benefit if the fuzzy matching is improved?
hat_prompts.green: What creative safeguard could catch mismatches before payroll runs?
hat_prompts.blue: What's the process to validate the fix before full rollout?

medium.title: The Client Satisfaction Dip
medium.scenario_text: Client satisfaction scores dropped a full point this quarter, right after the company took on three new large accounts. Leadership isn't sure if it's a service quality issue or simple growing pains.

hat_prompts.white: What data do we have that explains the drop?
hat_prompts.red: What's the team's gut feeling about why clients are less satisfied?
hat_prompts.black: What's the risk of taking on more clients before fixing this?
hat_prompts.yellow: What opportunity does this feedback create for improvement?
hat_prompts.green: What unconventional client check-in idea could help?
hat_prompts.blue: How should we structure an investigation into this drop?

medium.title: The Recruitment Surge
medium.scenario_text: Applications jumped from 210 to 340 in a single month after a viral LinkedIn post, but the recruitment team is now overwhelmed and response times are slipping.

hat_prompts.white: What are the actual numbers behind this surge?
hat_prompts.red: How does the recruitment team feel about the sudden workload?
hat_prompts.black: What could go wrong if candidates wait too long for responses?
hat_prompts.yellow: What's the upside of having this much interest in the company?
hat_prompts.green: What's a creative way to triage this surge quickly?
hat_prompts.blue: What should the short-term plan be this week?

medium.title: The Cross-Department Headcount Mismatch
medium.scenario_text: Three departments reported different headcount numbers for the same month, causing confusion in a leadership report. No one is sure which system is correct.

hat_prompts.white: What do the three conflicting numbers actually show?
hat_prompts.red: How does it feel to present a report you're not confident in?
hat_prompts.black: What's the risk of leadership losing trust in HR data?
hat_prompts.yellow: What's the benefit of finally reconciling these systems?
hat_prompts.green: What's a creative way to keep headcount data in sync going forward?
hat_prompts.blue: Who should own headcount reporting from now on?

medium.title: The Referral Bonus Debate
medium.scenario_text: A manager proposed a referral bonus program to boost quality hires, but Finance is worried it'll be abused with low-quality referrals just to collect the bonus.

hat_prompts.white: What do we know about how referral programs perform elsewhere?
hat_prompts.red: How do employees feel about the idea of earning a bonus this way?
hat_prompts.black: What's the real risk of bonus abuse?
hat_prompts.yellow: What's the upside if the program attracts great hires?
hat_prompts.green: What creative safeguard could prevent abuse without killing incentive?
hat_prompts.blue: What's the plan to pilot this before a full rollout?

hard.title: The HR Subdomain Merger
hard.scenario_text: Leadership wants to merge Employee Relations, Recruitment, and Organization Development into a single unified workflow to cut reporting overhead, but each subdomain has its own tools, habits, and institutional knowledge built over years.

hat_prompts.white: What are the concrete differences between how each subdomain currently works?
hat_prompts.red: What's the emotional undercurrent among staff in each subdomain about this merger?
hat_prompts.black: What institutional knowledge could be lost in a rushed merger?
hat_prompts.yellow: What efficiency could this merger genuinely unlock?
hat_prompts.green: What's an unconventional way to merge workflows without erasing what works?
hat_prompts.blue: What should the phased plan for this merger look like?

hard.title: The Silent Exit Wave
hard.scenario_text: Exit interviews over two quarters show 60% of departing employees cite 'unclear growth paths' as their main reason for leaving, but no one raised concerns in regular check-ins beforehand.

hat_prompts.white: What do the exit interview numbers actually say?
hat_prompts.red: Why might employees not have raised this concern earlier?
hat_prompts.black: What's the risk if this pattern continues unaddressed?
hat_prompts.yellow: What opportunity does clearer growth planning create for retention?
hat_prompts.green: What's a bold, new way to make growth paths visible to employees?
hat_prompts.blue: How should we structure a response across HR subdomains?

hard.title: The Automation Trust Gap
hard.scenario_text: After the timesheet and Excel automation tools saved hours of manual work, some senior staff still insist on manually double-checking every automated report, slowing everything back down.

hat_prompts.white: What evidence exists about the automation tool's accuracy so far?
hat_prompts.red: Why might senior staff feel uneasy trusting the automation?
hat_prompts.black: What's the real risk of full trust without any human check?
hat_prompts.yellow: What's the benefit if senior staff fully adopted the automation?
hat_prompts.green: What creative step could build trust in the tool faster?
hat_prompts.blue: What's the plan to transition from manual double-checking to trust?

hard.title: The Restructuring Rumor Mill
hard.scenario_text: A rumor is spreading that the tech stream will be absorbed into a different department, and no official statement has been made. Productivity is visibly dropping as people speculate.

hat_prompts.white: What is actually confirmed versus rumor at this point?
hat_prompts.red: What's the emotional toll of prolonged uncertainty on the team?
hat_prompts.black: What could go wrong if leadership stays silent much longer?
hat_prompts.yellow: What's the benefit of addressing this transparently now?
hat_prompts.green: What's a creative way to communicate uncertainty honestly?
hat_prompts.blue: Who should communicate next, and on what timeline?

hard.title: The Job Description Bottleneck
hard.scenario_text: Every job description now requires sign-off from three different HR subdomains before recruitment can post it, causing average time-to-post to double in the last quarter.

hat_prompts.white: What does the data show about time-to-post before and after this process?
hat_prompts.red: How does the recruitment team feel about the added delay?
hat_prompts.black: What's the risk of losing strong candidates due to slow posting?
hat_prompts.yellow: What's the benefit of the current sign-off process, if any?
hat_prompts.green: What's a creative way to streamline sign-off without losing oversight?
hat_prompts.blue: What should the new approval process look like?
